observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor issue an Order
immediately directing the Inventory Committee to conduct and complete the
inventory taking of all properties of the LGU by December 31, 2025, prepare
the RPCPPE and accordingly cleanse the PPE account of non-existent/missing
items. We further recommended that the Inventory Committee submit the
prepared RPCPPE to the Audit Team by January 31, 2026.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the correcting
journal entries relative to the understated depreciation of the affected PPEs
amounting to ₱148,292.16 based on the actual date of receipt of the assets as
indicated in the CTA/IAR upon its initial recognition and, prospectively
observe the same practice on subsequent purchases.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the LDRRMO and the
Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) to
revisit the LDRRMFIP and prospectively incorporate pre-identified PPAs
under the thematic areas of Disaster Response and Rehabilitation, and
Recovery.

We recommended that the MDRRMO, in coordination with the MPDC Office,
strengthen the selection process and prioritization of PPAs charged to the
LDRRMF through careful analysis and consideration of potential disaster
events or emerging hazardous situations that might threaten the LGU and its
corresponding capability to handle the risks of disasters and other hazards.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineering Office:
a. Issue the Certificate of Final Acceptance to the contractor after one
year from the date of project completion, upon determination by the
Inspectorate Team that no defects/failures were detected, or should
there be any, these were satisfactorily complied with by the contractor
concerned;
b. Require the contractors to post a warranty security against structural
defects upon the issuance of the Certificate of Final Acceptance, rather
than upon final payment. To ensure that the warranty security is posted,
we further recommend that the Municipality make it a policy to observe
the one (1) year period after completion of the project before returning the
retention money, unless substituted with a security, after which the
warranty period shall commence.
c. Henceforth, observe the prescribed periods provided in Section
62.2.3.2 on the imposition of the warranty security, which is 2 to 15
years after final acceptance depending on the type of the
infrastructure project.

We recommended that the GFPS TWG:
a. ensure that GAD-related PPAs are fully implemented during the year and
closely coordinate with the implementing offices to track progress and set
definite target dates for each activity; and
b. conduct periodic assessments to identify challenges encountered during
implementation and incorporate solutions into future proposals to
ensure the timely and full implementation of identified GAD PPAs
during the year.

1.11. We recommended that the Municipal Engineer investigate the status of the Municipal
Dumpsite with Drainage System of ₱1,500,750.00 and the Drainage System of
₱911,810.00, both recorded as Construction in Progress (CIP) to
determine its existence and remaining useful life, and prepare a report thereon
for submission to the Municipal Accountant.
1.12. We also recommended that the Appraisal Committee expedite its appraisal of
the mini-rescue vehicle to determine its fair value and subsequently submit a
report to the Municipal Accountant as a basis for recording in the books.
1.13. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the
appropriate journal entry on the projects recorded in the CIP account based
on the report of the Municipal Engineer and the donated mini-rescue vehicle based on the report of the Appraisal Committee.

2.6. We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Accountant prepare
the accounting entries to reclassify to the appropriate income accounts the amounts
comprising the amount of ₱265,351.64 which are inappropriately recorded in the Due
to NGAs account.

3.11. We reiterated our recommendation for the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Disbursement Voucher in favor of the Bureau of Treasury for the return of the dormant balances in the Due to NGAs account totaling ₱1,731,971.73 in the General Fund and ₱992,052.04 in the Trust Fund to extinguish the liability of the Municipality.

4.9. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, together with the members of the
Municipal Planning Development Council, through the MPD Coordinator:
a.) Resolve the problems encountered on the unimplemented PPAs in
CY2023 and prior years;
b.) Realign the appropriations of PPAs with unresolved issues and
reprogram to more viable and necessary PPAs;
c.) Implement all identified PPAs within specific time schedules of the
current year.

5.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, in coordination with the MDRRM Officer:

c. Include in the LDRRMF Investment Plan the activities to be funded from the unexpended LDRRMF from previous years which are in the Special Trust Fund; and

d. Stop allocating and using the LDRRMF for administrative expenses instead, provide sufficient budget to the MDRRM Office for their administrative needs.

6.5 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, in coordination with the
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer:
a. Transfer the amount of ₱148,379.47 under the ARCP II Loan Proceeds
and the ₱160,361.08 in the IRA-Tacloban Branch accounts to the
General Fund and close both dormant accounts;
b. Inform the Municipal Budget Officer once the funds are transferred
for inclusion in the budget and subsequent appropriation; and
c. Remit to the Bureau of Treasury the amount of ₱70,594.20 under KR2
Program to close the dormant account.

7.12 We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer specify in the budget the
funds intended for Peace and Order Programs (POP), under the approved
POPS Plan, from which the allocation for Confidential Funds shall be based.

7.13 We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the
Municipal Peace and Order Council, require the submission of the following
documents as fundamental pre-requisites in budgeting for Confidential Funds,
to wit:

c. Physical and Financial Plan which contains the estimated amount per
project, activity, and program; and

d. PNP Certification containing the concrete details of the peace and
order situation or circumstances that require the urgency in allocating
confidential funds.

8.5 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:

b.) inform the concerned payees that their checks were already stale and
require them to return the check for issuance of a new one or waive their
claims in favor of the LGU; and
c) furnish the Municipal Accountant with all documents resulting from the action taken on the stale checks.

8.6 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary
adjusting entry based on the report of the Municipal Treasurer on the stale
checks to restore the amount of cash previously recorded as disbursements.

1.19 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct and monitor the Inventory
Committee on their compliance with the guidelines and procedures in inventory
taking, recognition of those found at the station, and disposition of non-existing
and/ missing PPE items for one-time cleansing of PPE as prescribed in COA
Circular No. 2020-006.

1.1. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

a) Review the applicability of the residual values on infrastructure assets
in the computation of its depreciation. Otherwise, prepare an adjusting
entry to reflect the appropriate carrying values of the PPEs under the
Infrastructure Assets account without the incorporation of the residual
value in the computation of its depreciation.

b) Prepare a JEV to derecognize the recorded PPEs with an acquisition
cost below the capitalization threshold of ₱15,000.00 in accordance
with COA Circular 2020-006.

2.9. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare a JEV to revert to
Government Equity the long outstanding and undocumented payables
aggregating ₱2,442,007.94 in order to present in the books only the valid
liabilities.

3.13 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Disbursement
Voucher/s, and the Municipal Treasurer to issue the corresponding check/s,
for those amounts under the Due to NGAs account which remained idle and has no
details, and remit the same to the Bureau of Treasury, informing the Source
Agencies of such return.

3.14 Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant reclassify to the
appropriate accounts the balances which may not have been appropriately
recorded in the Due to NGAs account.

4.9. We recommended that the Municipality discontinue the utilization of its
LDRRM Fund for items/activities that are not related to disaster risk
reduction and management.

5.6. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require all winning contractors
to submit their respective CSHP to DOLE for approval prior to the
implementation of each infrastructure project.

6.10 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, together with the HRMO, revisit
the approved Organizational Structure and Staffing Pattern Plan and
consider filling up the vacant positions. Likewise, formulate written policies
relative to the hiring of Job Order personnel.

1.14. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entries to
reclassify the various inventory items in the PPE accounts and the
accumulated depreciation attributed to said items.

1.15. We also recommended that the Inventory Committee implement the
guidelines and procedures in inventory taking, recognition of those found at
the station, and disposition for non-existing and/ missing PPE items for one-
time cleansing of PPE as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2020-006 and
specifically to:
a) prepare and complete the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) containing
the specific assignments/duties of the Committee members, the cut-
off date, and a schedule specifying the dates and locations of the
inventory taking activities from start, up to the targeted completion
of physical inventory;
b) submit the approved PIP to the COA Audit Team at least ten (10)
calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory taking
activities;
c) conduct the inventory taking activities in accordance with the PIP,
ensuring all necessary information such as the cost and specific
identifiers like the dates and property numbers of the properties are
indicated, using the prescribed forms (attached as Annexes in this
AOM), of all properties of the Municipality over a three-month
period, starting on July 1, 2022;
d) prepare the RPCPPE upon completion of the physical count, adopting
the uniform property identification system for PPE, showing
complete data and clustered by PPE type, and submit the same to the
Audit Team on or before December 29, 2022.

2.12 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a) prepare the Journal Entry Voucher to reclassify the amount of
₱7,748,277.05 from Trust Liabilities – MDRRMF in the General Fund to
Government Equity account; and
b) prepare the Disbursement Voucher to transfer the CY 2020 unexpended
balances of the QRF and DRRMF – MOOE in the aggregate amount of
₱2,366,613.87 from the General Fund to the Trust Fund; and make such
transfer of any unexpended balances of both QRF and DRRMF – MOOE
to the Trust Fund every year end.

3.15. We recommended, prospectively, that the MDRRM Council, through the
MRDDRM Officer, identify DRRM programs/projects/activities which can be
implemented during the year to maximize the utilization of the DRRM Fund.

4.15. We recommended, prospectively, that the Municipal Planning and
Development Council, through the MPDO, identify priority projects which
can be implemented during the year to maximize the utilization of its 20%
Development Fund.

5.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Heads of Offices, who
were designated incharge with the implementation of the programs funded by
various National Government Agencies, to submit a certification to the
Municipal Accountant that there are no on-going programs funded by said
agencies.

5.9 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the DV, and
the Municipal Treasurer issue the checks in favor of the Bureau of Treasury
for the return of the unutilized funds from NGAs totaling ₱2,388,165.53.

6.11 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer submit to the Audit Team the
lacking supporting documents mentioned in paragraph 6.9 to validate the
eligibility and entitlement of the claimants who received the SRA and AHDP
totaling P880,063.65.

6.12 Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant record in the
books of the LGU the Liquidation Reports on cash advances only if these are
supported with complete pertinent documents.

7.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, and the Department Heads
concerned, plan meticulously and judiciously all procurement for catering
services and incorporate in the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) the targetted
dates and modes of procurement.

7.10 Further, we recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee cease the
practice of splitting and adopt public bidding or the alternative mode of
procurement when the conditions are met, to avoid audit disallowances.

1.14. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue a directive to the
Inventory Committee to:
a.) Prepare the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) and submit to the Audit
Team after the same has been approved by the Municipal Mayor
but before the scheduled start of inventory-taking activities;
b.) Start immediately, and complete during the year, the physical
count of all PPEs of the Municipality, including road networks, in
accordance with the procedures and schedule in the PIP as
prescribed in COA Circular No. 2020-006 and other relevant
issuance; and
c.) Prepare the corresponding RPCPPE and RPCLRN, and submit
the same to the Accountant for reconciliation and to the Audit
Team for verification.

1.15. We also recommended that the Property Custodian prepare and
maintain Property Cards for each class of PPE, and LRNPC for all
roads and its components, based on the RPCPPE and RPCLRN,
respectively.

1.16. Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a.) Prepare and maintain the PPELC for each class of PPE, and
LRNLC for each local road, and reconcile these with the
RPCPPE and RPCLRN, respectively; and
b.) Start the cleansing of PPE accounts by following the procedures
in COA Circular No. 2020-006 and based on the RPCPPE and
RPCLRN.

1.17. In their reply to the Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM), the
Inventory Team informed the Audit Team that they have convened and
initially conducted the physical count of PPE in CY 2020. However, it
was interrupted by various activities, events organized and facilitated by
the Inventory Team. It was also postponed due to the surge of COVID-19
cases. They appreciate and acknowledge the audit observation and will
immediately act and prepare the necessary report, as recommended by the
Audit Team.

2.13. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a.) Submit the copies of cancelled and paid checks for the months of
October to December 2020, to the Audit Team to facilitate the
timely verification of the monthly BRS.
b.) Prepare a Journal Entry Voucher to cancel the stale checks and
restore the amount to the Cash in Bank-LCCA account and set-up
the corresponding liability accounts.

2.14. In reply to the AOM, the Municipal Account submitted other documents
to support the October to December 2020 BRS but copies of the encashed
checks were not included. He also informed the Audit Team that the
necessary entries were made in the books in CY 2021 to adjust the stale
checks, but copies of the JEV were not submitted yet.

3.12. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue a directive to:
a.) all Heads of Offices in the LGU instructing that receipt and
deliveries of all procured supplies and materials should be
centralized and handled only by the Supply Office for monitoring
and proper recording of deliveries by the said office; and
b.) the Inventory Committee to undertake a complete physical
inventory of all supplies and materials, and submit the Report on
the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to the Audit Team and
the Municipal Accountant every January 31 and July 31 to
ascertain the accuracy of the Inventory account balance.

3.13. We also recommended that, in subsequent procurement, the Supply
Officer:
a.) Issue a pre-numbered approved Requisition and Issue Slip upon
release of any kind of supplies and materials to the end-users;
b.) Prepare the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) duly
supported with pre-numbered RIS issued during each month and
Distribution Lists prepared by end-user departments, and submit
the same to the Municipal Accountant every 1st week of the
following month to reduce his accountability.

3.14. Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant record in
the appropriate Inventory account all supplies and materials upon
purchase, except those purchased out of the petty cash fund, and
record the related expense only when supported with the Report of
Supplies and Materials Issued accompanied with pre-numbered
Requisition and Issue Slips and Distribution List from the end-users
for inventories held for distribution.

4.13. We recommended that the Chairman and members of the Bids and
Awards Committee:
c.) Indicate always in the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) the
appropriate mode of procurement; and
d.) Require the BAC Secretariat in subsequent procurement, to post
in the GPPB online portal the updated APP, Notice of Award and
66
other information relative to the PPAs procured through
Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act.

4.14. As stated in their reply to the AOM, the BAC Chairman had already
directed the BAC Secretariat to revise the APP of the affected
procurement items, to indicate the proper procurement modality. They
also instructed the BAC Secretariat to post in the GPPB Portal, the
updated APP, NOA, and PO for procurements through Emergency
Procurement under Bayanihan Act modality.

5.8. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the MDRRM
Officer:
c.) Obtain from the MDRRM Council and the Sangguniang
Bayan authority for the disbursements for COVID-19-
related expenditures in CY 2020 totaling P2,361,545.00
which were not incorporated in the revised LDRRMF
Investment Plan and the Supplemental Investment Plan;
and
d.) Incorporate in the annual/revise LDRRMF Investment
Plan and the Annual/Supplemental Investment Plan, all
subsequent disbursements to be charged to LDRRMF, with
proper authority from the MDRRM Council and the
Sangguniang Bayan,

5.9. We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor authorize, and
the Municipal Budget and Municipal Accountant process payments,
for the utilization of the LDRRMF only for programs, projects and
activities which are included in the LDRRMF Investment Plan to
ensure the legality of the transactions.

6.10. We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee, and its
Secretariat, require contractors of subsequent procurement contracts
of the Municipality to post the Performance Security and strictly
comply with Sections 39 and 54.5 of the 2016 R-IRR of RA 9184 to
guarantee the faithful performance by the contractor of its obligations
under the contract.

6.11. The BAC Chairman replied that during the bid evaluation, they found out
that the submitted Omnibus Sworn Statement was not for Emergency
Procurement under Bayanihan to Heal as One Act. Hence, the Technical
Working Group instructed the bidder to submit a new copy using the
format prescribed under GPPB Circular 01-2020. The bidder was able to
submit the required document during the post-qualification period but, due
to inadvertence, the document attached to the contract was the one
previously submitted by the bidder.

6.12. All other recommendations related thereto were duly noted by the BAC
and commits to comply so as not to put the Procuring Entity at a
disadvantage.

7.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue a directive to the
Department Heads of the Municipality to prepare a report on
subsequent donations that will be received by their offices, whether in
cash or in-kind, and submit the report to the Municipal Treasurer or
the Supply Officer-Designate, for subsequent submission to the
Municipal Accountant.

7.8 We also recommended that the Supply Officer-Designate prepare a
Consolidated Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and
Balances based on the reports submitted by the Heads of Departments
of the Municipality and furnish the Municipal Accountant and the
Audit Team with copies of the said report pursuant to COA Circular
No. 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020.

8.11 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor stop the grant of cash
advances to personnel who are not bonded and are not holders of
permanent appointment.

8.12 We also recommended that, henceforth, the Municipal Accountant
and Municipal Treasurer, pay suppliers through check and ensure
that all cash advances are supported with the required necessary
documents.

8.13 The Municipal Accountant stated in his reply to the AOM that no cash
advance was granted since January 1, 2021. The observation is noted for
implementation the whole year of 2021.

8.14 The Acting Municipal Treasurer added that for the internal control of
disbursement of funds of the Municipality, the recommendation is noted
and for this year 2021, will pay the suppliers through check and ensure
that all cash advances are made by bonded permanent personnel.

9.9. We recommended that the Municipal Engineer submit to the Audit
Team the Inspection Report on the projects Fabrication of Steel
Frame Divider and Construction of Septic Tank to avoid the issuance of
Notice of Suspension or Disallowance.

Local Government Support Fund (LGSF)
The Municipality of Capoocan received ₱500,000.00 on December 17, 2020,
through Notice of Authority to Debit Account Issued (NADAI) from the Bureau of the
Treasury, the Local Government Support Fund (LGSF) COVID-19 Financial Assistance
for the food expenses of LSIs, PUIs and PUMs in Quarantine/Isolation Facilities. The
amount was requested by the LGU from the national government, through a letter dated
October 28, 2020, to be implemented in CY 2021. The funds were unutilized as of
December 31, 2020.

1.11 We recommended that the:
a. Inventory Committee conduct a physical inventory of the local roads
under the LGU’s jurisdiction, reconcile the list of inventory per local
road inventory report and road map with those recorded in the Road
Networks account, and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of
Local Road Network;
b. General Services Officer render a Report on Local Road Network;
c. Municipal Accountant disclose pertinent information on road network
system in the Notes to Financial Statements, compute the correct
depreciation expense for the road networks, and prepare the necessary
adjusting entries.

2.18 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review and verify the
stale checks which are still included as outstanding checks in the Bank
Reconciliation Statement and prepare the necessary adjusting entry in order to
reflect the accurate balance of the Cash in Bank Local Currency Current
Account in the books of the Municipality. Likewise the Municipal Treasurer
refrain from releasing checks to persons other than the payee or his duly
authorized representative in accordance with Local Treasury Operations
Manual.
2.19 We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal
Accountant obtain necessary documents to establish the existence, validity and
accuracy of the bank balance from Philippine Postal Savings Bank, and submit
to the Audit Team thereafter.

3.11 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive create a team that will
closely monitor the implementation of projects and assist in the liquidation
of fund transfers.
3.12 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant send demand
letters to the barangays to liquidate the fund transfers, and submit the Report
of Checks Issued, Report of Disbursements (ROD) and other documents to
support the liquidation to the Audit Team and strictly require the implementing
agency to submit monthly, the ROD as provided in COA Circular 94-013.

4.14 We recommended that the:
a. BAC and Municipal Engineer properly plan and schedule all
procurement activities pertaining to development projects for its
timely implementation and completion;
b. Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator as well as the
Municipal Engineer strictly monitor and implement the programs,
projects and activities within the project duration as stated in the budget
proposal. Likewise, conduct intensive review of the projects funded
under the 20% DF – continuing appropriation, to determine if those are
still part of the vital needs of the Municipality, otherwise, reprogram it
to other priority projects.
c. Municipal Budget Officer prepare a report of the balances from
completed development projects and request the Sangguniang Bayan
through the Local Development Council to revert and re-appropriate the
said balances.

5.18 We recommended that the Municipality discontinue utilizing its
LDRRM Fund for items/activities that are not related with the four thematic
areas of disaster risk reduction and management.
5.19 We also recommended that the LDRRMC prepare the list of PPAs to be
funded from the prior years’ unexpended balances of LDRRM Fund and
incorporate it in the current year LDRRMFIP to maximize its use; and indicate
in the AIP the breakdown of PPAs for the 70% LDRRM Fund.

6.8 We recommended that the Solid Waste Management Board prioritize the
development of a sanitary landfill site pursuant to Republic Act 9003 or the
“Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000.”
6.9 We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) require the barangays concerned to properly maintain the MRF
and conduct periodic monitoring of waste collection activities; and
b) improve coordination with the barangays for designing effective and
efficient waste collection system.

7.11 We recommended that the Local Finance Committee:
a) apply a systematic and realistic forecasting of revenue, taking into
account prior years’ experience and historical data as aid in setting
targets in order to establish a realizable estimated revenue; and
b) conduct periodic review and monitoring to ascertain whether
collection targets are attained, and expedite the revision of the Local
Revenue Code and the creation of the Market and Cemetery Code.
7.12 We also recommended that the Office of the Municipal Treasurer
intensify its collection capability in order to at least achieve the targeted revenue.

8.10 We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer in coordination
with the Municipal Accountant, immediately remit all contributions of the Job
Order personnel to the Pag-IBIG Fund, through the preparation of
disbursement voucher and issuance of check.
8.11 Likewise, the Municipal Accountant draw a journal entry voucher to
reclassify the Pag-IBIG contributions recorded in the Accounts Payable account
to Due to Pag-IBIG account amounting to ₱11,300.00.

9.12 We recommended that the GFPS shall conduct gender analysis of their
attributed programs and projects through the administration of the HGDG tool
during the project identification and preparation of GAD AR to determine
whether the attributed GAD PPAs entirely address gender related issues.
Likewise, the Municipality shall establish, enhance, strengthen and maintain a
gender responsive evaluation system to monitor the implementation of GAD
PPAs.

We recommended that the accounting and reporting guidelines for the local road network construction and maintenance system as prescribed in COA
Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 be strictly adhered to, thus
Management should:
a) Comply with the transitory provision in recording local roads in the
books of account;
b) Compute the correct depreciation expense and make necessary
adjustments; and
c) Require the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of
the local roads and submit the Report on the Physical Count of the
Road Network System. Likewise, enjoin the General Services
Officer to maintain Local Road Network Property Card, and
render a Report on Local Road Network.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the
Municipal Accountant to conduct thorough and final review, analysis and
reconciliation of all dormant accounts pursuant to the Guidelines and
Procedures on the Write-off of Dormant Receivable Accounts, Unliquidated
Cash Advances, and Fund Transfers of NGAs, LGUs and GOCCs, as prescribed
by COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

Likewise, consider the improvement of the existing clauses in the MOA
and submit it to the Office of the Auditor within five (5) days from its execution.
Also, attach a Certification issued by the Municipal Accountant that funds
previously transferred to the barangays have been liquidated and accounted for
in the books.

We recommended that the accounting personnel be reminded to
faithfully perform their duties pursuant to Section 444, Sub-section b.1,
paragraph x of RA 7160. Thus, require the Municipal Accountant to submit the
copies of bank statements, cancelled and paid checks, debit/credit memos and
all other supporting documents necessary to facilitate the verification of
monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer, Municipal Planning
and Development Officer and Municipal Agriculturist be required to fast-tract
the implementation of programs and projects in accordance with the timeliness
upon which the projects should be implemented or completed as provided for in
the guidelines and project proposals. Likewise, require the Municipal Social
Welfare and Development Officer to immediately release to the qualified
beneficiaries the remaining balance of funds for the Emergency Shelter
Assistance. Moreover, consider the return of the unutilized funds of
completed/long unimplemented projects to the Source Agencies.

We recommended that Management ensure that programs, projects
and activities (PPAs) that are to be funded out of the 20% development plan be
clearly specified in the Annual Investment Plan. We also recommend that the
Municipal Planning & Development Officer and Budget Officer be required to
evaluate the PPAs included in the continuing appropriation and propose its
reversion and appropriation for priority eligible projects indicated in Item 3 of
DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommended that the Management comply with Section 21 of R.A
10121 in the allocation of the required minimum amount to be set aside for
LDRRMF in order to have sufficient funds in carrying out its disaster risk
reduction and management programs, projects and activities, and attain the
objective of the law.

We recommended that the Solid Waste Management Board: (a)
facilitate the establishment of a sanitary landfill site; (b) make representations
with the concerned barangays of the Municipality thru the Municipal Mayor, to
properly maintain the Materials Recovery Facility and strictly implement the
“No Segregation, No Collection Policy” at source; and (c) fast track the
establishment of electrical connection to the dump site so that the purchased
machines will be operational to minimize the residual solid waste of the
Municipality, and properly maintain the residual containment area.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer be directed to
immediately remit the premium contributions and loan amortizations to the
Home Development Mutual Fund, and henceforth, strictly observe the prompt
remittances of the same in accordance with Section 3(a) of the IRR of RA 9679.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to draw a
journal entry voucher to correct the entry reclassifying the Due to Pag-IBIG
account to Accounts Payable account.

We recommended that henceforth, Management execute a Contract of
Service or Memorandum of Agreement when hiring Job Order (JO) personnel,
stating therein the duties and responsibilities, expected outputs and the rate per
day of the workers. Likewise, involve the human resource department in the
selection and hiring of job-order personnel.

The job order personnel shall likewise be required to register their
attendance through bundy clock/biometrics.

We recommended that Management should strictly implement the
provisions of Municipal Ordinance No. 309, s. 2016 or the Revenue Code of the
Municipality of Capoocan so that amount of business taxes and rental of burial
lots are correctly assessed and collected.

We recommended that the Municipality develop and maintain a GAD
Database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data and
other GAD-related information which will serve as basis for gender analysis,
planning, programming and policy formulation.

We also recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development
Office (MPDO), with the assistance of the GFPS, undertake gender analysis to
identify and confirm existing gender issues, and ensure that the GAD PPAs are
responsive to the said issues.

We recommend that management file the request for authority to write-
off dormant accounts totaling ₱374,304.34 to the COA Audit Team pursuant
to Section 8 of COA Circular No. 2016-005, dated December 19, 2016.

It is emphasized that the above-mentioned Circular shall not be interpreted
to neither condone the written off accounts nor to extinguish the obligations. The
Management shall continue to exert effort to collect the accounts when
circumstances would warrant. Further, this shall not be construed as a ground to
exonerate the liability of the officers/employees for infidelity in the custody of
documents.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive create an inventory
committee, who shall take charge of the annual inventory-taking and to
submit a report thereon. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant and the
Municipal Treasurer to prepare the PPE ledger cards and property cards,
respectively.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant strictly
observe the timely submission of the accounts in accordance with Section 347
of RA 7160, Section 7.2.1 (a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September
15, 2009 and Section 39 of PD 1445.

We recommend that the BAC and Municipal Accountant make
representation with the concerned contractors to post warranty securities for
the infrastructure projects that had already been fully paid. Henceforth,
strictly observe the mandatory posting of warranty security to guarantee that
the contractor shall perform his responsibilities as prescribed in Section
62.2.3.1 of IRR of RA 9184 and Section 9.1.1.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001
dated June 14, 2012.

We recommend that management immediately submit the lacking
documents and henceforth, strictly comply with the documents required by
COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) be
required to prepare Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR), and furnish the
COA Audit Team with a copy thereof, which shall serve as an effective tool in
the systematic and logical assessment/evaluation of the efficiency and
effectiveness of the Municipality’s Procurement System. Likewise, strictly
comply with all the documentary requirements and observe correct
application of the procedures in the conduct of competitive bidding, as
outlined in the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive propose to the
Sangguniang Bayan, for the creation of the required LDRRM Office and
appropriation for the salaries and wages of its personnel, as well as other
budgetary requirements of the office.

We recommend that the Local School Board ensure that the
preparation and approval of the SEF Budget for the ensuing year should be
within the timeline or schedule provided in DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint
Circular No. 1, s. 2017 dated January 19, 2017 and see to it that all
programs/projects and activities identified in the budget be implemented as
planned, to meet the goals set and deliver effective service to the public.

We recommend that Management stop the practice of splitting
purchase requisitions or contracts, pursuant to COA Circular No. 76-41 dated
July 30, 1976, and strictly adhere to the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA
9184, to promote effective competition and transparency in the
procurement process and obtain a fair and equitable contract for the best
interest of the government.

We recommend that management exercise prudence in planning its
activity and observe austerity measures in disbursing government funds to
avoid incurrence of extravagant expenditures.

We recommend that the officer in charge of payroll preparation be
advised to strictly observe the limitations on payroll deductions so that the
monthly net take home pay of employees will not be reduced to an amount
lower that Four Thousand Pesos (P 4000.00) pursuant to Section 47 of the
General Provisions of General Appropriations Act of FY 2017.

We recommend that management create or establish a GAD Focal
Point System (GFPS) who will prepare and submit the GAD Plan and Budget,
as well as the Accomplishment Report, pursuant to the pertinent provisions of
PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

observations.managementAction: The verifications of these DVs are still on-going.
No cash advance policy letters. liquidation letters.
The Municipal Accountant submitted 212 DVs with a total amount of P11,097,004.47.
The verifications of these DVs are still on-going.

The Municipal Accountant explained that these are prior years’ balances which need verification and validation. As soon as the office finalizes the process, the audit recommendation to request for dropping or writing-off of accounts shall be undertaken.
The Municipal Accountant submitted 268 DVs with a total amount of P5,407,047.81 leaving a balance of 128 DVs with a total amount of P4,985,795.21

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC to follow strictly Section 3.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.reason: Still the management was not able to submit approved Purchase Orders (POs) within five (5) working days from the issuance thereof.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LCE direct his concerned official to facilitate the hiring of a new Municipal Engineer or request the assistance of the Provincial Engineer’s Office in the implementation of their projects.
observations.managementAction: explained that they were not able to facilitate the hiring of a new Municipal Engineer or request the Provincial Engineer; they have no Municipal Engineer.
observations.reason: They were not able to facilitate the hiring of a new Municipal Engineer or request the assistance of the Provincial Engineer’s Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to maintain the Property Cards and the files of ARE or designate a Property Officer to take charge of the LGU’s properties. Also, we recommend that all those personnel issued IT Equipment with multiple issued units return them immediately to the Municipal Treasurer/Property Officer for accounting and safekeeping.
observations.reason: The Municipality was not able to submit; no action taken.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Head of Health Services Department to submit or furnish copies of donated equipment to the Property and Accounting Office for recording purposes and for other appropriate actions.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant explained that he was not able to record the donated equipment due to lack of cost basis.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant was not able to record the donated equipment due to lack of cost basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LCE direct the officials and employees concerned to refund the excess payment of the incentive and stop the practice of granting incentives or whatever form of benefits in excess of what is allowed.
observations.reason: No action taken by the management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to exert all efforts to verify the nature of the receivable and send demand letters to enforce collection thereof. For receivables that cannot be substantiated with documents, request authority from COA to drop or write-off the amount from the books of accounts of the LGU.
observations.reason: Balances need verification and validation; recommendation to drop or write-off to be undertaken when process finalized.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to secure comprehensive fire insurance from GSIS on all government properties to provide adequate protection from possible risk of loss or damage due to calamities like fire, typhoon, earthquake or other casualties.
observations.reason: Not included in the budget.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend to the Management:
(a) That purchases of supplies and materials shall be coursed thru inventory
account and issuance thereof shall be recorded based on the duly accomplished
monthly Report of Supplies and Materials Issued in compliance with the
perpetual method used by the government in recording purchases of
inventories.
(b) Instruct the Property/Supply Officer to prepare RIS and consolidate the
supplies and materials issued using the SSMI and submit to the Municipal
Accountant’s Office to support the dropping from the books of all
issued/consumed supplies and materials. In addition, the Supply Officer
should maintain stock cards to account the receipt and disposition of all
supplies which reconcile with the ledger card of the Accounting Office.
(c) Prepare the necessary adjustment to reflect the actual correct balances of the
inventory accounts in compliance with the PPSAS requirements under PPSAS
No. 12 using the perpetual inventory method. Likewise, both records of the
Municipal Accountant and Property/Supply Officer should be adjusted
accordingly in order to present fairly the Financial Statements of the
Municipality.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive consider and prioritize the conduct
of physical inventory count of the Municipality property, plant and equipment in
the charge of an Inventory Committee and cause the submission of the Report of
the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to establish the existence,
propriety and accuracy of the reported balances of PPE in the financial
statements.

We also recommend that the Property Custodian and the Accounting Office
maintain property cards and property, plant and equipment ledger cards,
respectively, and conduct a periodic reconciliation of the property and accounting
records to bring both records into agreement.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards
Committee to adhere strictly to the provisions of the Revised Implementing
Rules and Regulations of RA 9184.

This observation is without prejudice to the issuance of the Notice of Suspension (NS)
and/or Notice of Disallowance (ND), as evidence warrants.

We recommend that the Municipal Engineer adhere strictly with DPWH D.O. No.
22 dated February 18, 2015 in the preparation and calculation of the Approved
Budget for the Contract (ABC). Likewise, the members of the Municipal
Engineering Office and the Technical Working Group of the Bids and Awards
Committee familiarize themselves with the promulgations issued by the DPWH
particularly for infrastructure projects.

This observation is without prejudice to the issuance of the Notice of Suspension (NS)
and/or Notice of Disallowance (ND), as evidence warrants.

We recommend that Management attend procurement trainings and seminars duly
conducted by the GPPB Regional Composite Team.

This observation is without prejudice to the issuance of the Notice of Suspension (NS)
and/or Notice of Disallowance (ND), as evidence warrants.

We recommend the full remittance of the above RPT shares and barangay aids to
the concerned barangays in order to prevent its use for other purpose in the
operation of the municipality and to enable the barangays the immediate
appropriation of the funds for projects/programs beneficial to the Barangay
constituents. Henceforth, see to it that their shares from RPT collections should be
remitted regularly within the prescribed period as provided under Section 271 of
RA 7160.

We recommend that the identified balances be transferred to the Special Trust Fund
account of the LGU in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002
and Section 6.4 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No.
2013-01.

We recommend that Management refrain from charging non priority or non-
related expenses to the Special Education Fund and strictly comply with the
provisions of Section 272 of R.A. 7160 and Joint Circular No. 1 s. 1998 of DECS,
DBM and DILG. Likewise, the Chairman and other Local School Board officials
should prioritize projects and activities that address the needs for quality
education of its constituents.

This observation is without prejudice to the issuance of the Notice of Suspension (NS)
and/or Notice of Disallowance (ND), as evidence warrants.

We recommend strict compliance of the procedural guidelines on the grant of CNA
Incentive as set forth under PSLMC Resolution No. 04, s. 2002 and DBM Budget
Circular No. 2016-7 dated December 1, 2016.

This observation is without prejudice to the issuance of the Notice of Suspension (NS)
and/or Notice of Disallowance (ND), as evidence warrants.

We recommend that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board fast track the
preparation of Solid Waste Management Plan and submit to the National Solid
Waste Management Commission for approval as prescribed under R.A. No. 9003
to ensure the efficient management of solid waste generated within its jurisdiction.

We recommend that the Management optimally utilize or endeavor to fully
implement the various projects, programs and activities mentioned in the AIP
funded under 20% Development Fund in each given year to ensure the attainment
of social-economic development and environmental outcomes in accordance with
DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

Likewise, Management should also monitor the implementation of the identified
priority projects, programs and activities that are to be funded out of the 20%
Development Fund in line with the proposals of the local development committee
that should regularly convene to update or modify the priority programs as called
for under existing circumstances and changing conditions.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should cautiously direct the Municipal Treasurer to be preparing the RCIs. In case the preparation is delegated to one of her staff, she should review the report first before certifying to the correctness of the report by affixing her signature.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to strictly adhere to the provision of Section 7.2.1 (a) of the 2009 Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Accounts prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009. Direct also the timely and complete submission of the accounts.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit the cancelled checks together with the Monthly Report of Checks Issued. He should also direct the submission of the 52 cancelled checks to the audit team.
observations.reason: Some cancelled checks were already submitted to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LCE direct the Bids and Awards Committee to adhere strictly to the provisions of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184.
observations.reason: Reiterated in the 2016 Annual Audit Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LCE direct the Bids and Awards Committee or the Municipal Accountant and Supply Officer-Designate to comply with the requirements under the R-IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: The BAC explained that the procurement process, however had compliantly submitted the documents cited in the deficiencies in the conduct of the related bidding process to the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: Some cancelled checks were already submitted to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee to require the participants to canvass and/or confirm their attendance to Capability Enhancement Trainings, Program Seminar conducted at Cebu Prince Hotel so as not to over-estimate the number of participants.
observations.reason: BAC recommend for management to review for bidding and/or in holding a management lead training and/or CapDev activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and Supply Officer-Designate to cautiously review supporting documents before transaction is passed for payment.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant committed to subscribed to applicable measures.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LCE direct the Chairman, Bids and Award Committee, the Supply Officer-Designate and the Municipal Accountant to adhere strictly with the provisions of R-IRR of RA 9184.
observations.reason: BAC promised to adhere strictly with the provisions of RA 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LCE direct the BAC and the Supply Officer-Designate to strictly adhere to Section 54.1 of R-IRR of RA 9184.
observations.reason: BAC promised to adhere strictly with the provisions of RA 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LCE direct the Bids and Awards Committee to conduct the evaluation of bids to ensure the correctness of quantity and prices accepted, and to disqualify items which are above the ABC so as not to overpay the procurement of goods.
observations.managementAction: Committed to conduct the evaluation of bids to ensure the correctness of quantity and prices accepted, and to disqualify items which are above the ABC.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU comply with all the requirements of COA Circular No. 2013-004 regarding guidelines on adequate information and publicity on Programs/Projects/Activities of the Government Agencies for transparency and accountability.

We recommend that: a) Management sets its internal guidelines on the preparation of the PPMP; b) The Municipal Budget Officer and the BAC Chairman ensure that the required PPMP are consolidated in the APP; c) Management strictly comply with the provisions of Sections 7.2 up to 7.3.4 of the IRR of RA 9184 in the preparation and submission of PPMP.
observations.managementAction: Management sets internal guidelines and the BAC committed to comply with the preparation of PPMP.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LCE direct the formation of the Municipal Waste Management Board that shall prepare, submit and implement a plan for the safe and sanitary management of solid waste generated in areas under geographic and political coverage as prescribed in Section 12 of RA 9003.
observations.reason: Reiterated in the 2016 Annual Audit Report.

observations.recommendation: We reiterate the 2013 Annual Audit Report (AAR) that the Municipal Mayor: (a) Create a committee to conduct the annual physical count of all PPE and to prepare and submit the RPCPPE; (b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile records of the PPE accounts with that of RPCPPE; and (c) Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the I and I Report on the Unserviceable Properties prior to the conduct of disposal, if any.
observations.reason: Not Implemented; reiterated in the 2016 Annual Audit Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor enforce strict letters and require officials and employees, elective and appointive, to immediately settle or liquidate their outstanding cash advances. Withhold the payment of salaries and emoluments of these officials and employees until the outstanding cash advances have been settled.
observations.reason: Policy and implementation pending; submission of liquidation report and refund in the excess of cash advances as the case maybe.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor enforce the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to strictly adhere to Section 7.2.1 (a) of the 2009 Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Accounts prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009. See to it that all DVs pertaining to a particular period are submitted completely and on time to COA Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: already submitted 212 DVs with corresponding total amount
observations.reason: There were already 268 DVs submitted with corresponding amount; balance remained.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to put into writing the reasons for not certifying and/or certifying under protest on each of the DV in question and attach same as one of the supporting documents as well as refrain from issuing the Accountant’s Advice in case of doubt.
observations.reason: The verifications of these DVs are still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to improve RPT tax collection of the Municipality. Enforce collection, otherwise, apply all remedies provided in Section 256 Chapter 6 of RA 7160.
observations.reason: Enforce collection; otherwise, apply all remedies provided in Section 256 Chapter 6 of RA 7160.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to strictly adhere to Section 7.2.1 (a) of the 2009 Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Accounts prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009. See to it that all DVs pertaining to a particular period are submitted completely and on time to COA Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: There were 268 DVs submitted with corresponding total amount of P11,097,004.47; balance remained.
observations.reason: There were 268 DVs submitted; balance of 128 DVs amounting to P4,985,795.21 remained.
